methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $13M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Terra Industries is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Nathan Nwachuku that has raised $12.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public peers in the defense technology sector, including Series A companies Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M), alongside Seed-stage peer Tenna Systems ($60M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-Progression anchors to the $12.6M raised as a proxy for implied current value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $31.5M. Peer-Median uses the four comparable companies cited above; the median valuation across this cohort is $225M, with a range of $60M to $500M. This wider spread reflects stage heterogeneity within the comp set—three peers are Series A, one is Seed.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $27M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $128M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around which stage comparables best reflect Terra's position: the Stage-Progression method implies $32M, assuming minimal market validation, while Peer-Median anchors to later-stage valuations that may not yet apply. Key variables include product maturity, customer traction, and team depth relative to the Series A cohort.
